title: Black Box Data After a Semi Crash in Vancouver, Washington
twitter: 🚛 Semi crash in Vancouver, WA? The truck's BLACK BOX could win your case — but data gets overwritten FAST. Don't wait. Call 855-WRECKMATCH (855-897-3256) now or visit https://www.wreckmatch.com/blog/black-box-data-after-a-semi-crash-in-vancouver-washington #TruckAccident #VancouverWA
linkedin:
If you or a client were involved in a semi-truck accident in Vancouver, Washington, one critical piece of evidence is often overlooked until it's too late: the truck's black box.

Commercial trucks are equipped with Electronic Logging Devices (ELDs) and Event Data Recorders (ECMs) that capture speed, braking patterns, hours of service, and driver behavior in the moments before a crash. This data can be the difference between a strong claim and an uphill battle — but here's the problem: trucking companies are not legally obligated to preserve it indefinitely, and overwrite cycles can erase it within days or weeks.

Washington State's 2-year statute of limitations may seem like plenty of time, but black box data does not wait that long.

For attorneys in our 800+ law firm network, WreckMatch connects truck accident victims with qualified legal counsel quickly — so preservation letters and spoliation holds can be filed before evidence disappears.

If you're working a Vancouver, WA truck accident case or need a referral partner who takes evidence timelines seriously, let's connect.

👉 Submit a case or learn more: https://www.wreckmatch.com/#form

Commercial trucks carry black boxes — called ELDs and ECMs — that record speed, hard braking, driver hours, and what happened in the seconds before impact. But that data can be overwritten in days or weeks, and once it's gone, it's gone.

Washington gives you 2 years to file a claim, but the black box won't wait 2 years. Acting fast with the right legal help can mean the difference between a case built on solid evidence and one built on guesswork.

reddit_title: Semi-truck crash in Vancouver, WA — does the truck's black box data help your case and how do you preserve it?
reddit_body:
If you were hit by a semi-truck in Vancouver, Washington, one of the most valuable pieces of evidence is the truck's onboard black box — specifically the Electronic Logging Device (ELD) and the Engine Control Module (ECM). These systems record speed, braking, throttle position, hours of service, and driver behavior in the moments leading up to a crash. In serious truck accident cases, this data can directly prove negligence.

The catch is timing. Trucking companies are not required to preserve this data indefinitely, and many ECM systems overwrite older data within 30 days or less. Washington State does give you a 2-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims, but waiting even a few weeks to take action could mean that critical black box evidence is already gone. An attorney can send a spoliation letter or seek an emergency preservation order to lock that data down before it disappears.

If you are dealing with a truck accident case in the Vancouver, WA area and are not sure where to start, it is worth speaking with a truck accident attorney as soon as possible — many work on contingency so there is no upfront cost. Resources like wreckmatch.com can help connect you with attorneys who specifically handle commercial truck cases and understand the urgency around evidence preservation.
